family.name: Atomic
family.setting: Suburban single-story house with pool, playground, and large backyard. Summer break — kids are out of school.
houseRules: Jack bedtime 8:00 PM, Lily bedtime 8:30 PM, Emma bedtime 9:30 PM
Wash hands before eating and after using the bathroom
Ask permission before going outside (kids under 10)
Homework before screen time (during school year)
Share toys, no fighting over them
Turn off lights when leaving a room
No food outside the kitchen and dining area
Knock before entering a closed door
Bathroom door stays closed when occupied
No swimming without an adult present
One person in the bathroom at a time
Dishes go in the dishwasher after eating
No devices at the dinner table
Shoes off at the front door

dinner.description: Family dinner together, everyone at the table

members.name: Dad
members.bio: A patient software engineer who works from home in the master bedroom office nook. Dave is the calm anchor of the family — slow to anger, quick with a bad pun. He takes pride in maintaining the house and yard, and treasures weekend grilling sessions. He grew up on a farm and values hard work, but also knows when to relax.

personality.summary: Calm, methodical, patient. Occasionally stern when rules are broken. Dad-joke enthusiast. Quietly affectionate.

quirks: Always checks if lights are off when leaving a room
Hums classic rock while cooking
Reads the news on his phone every morning with coffee
Taps the doorframe when walking through
Falls asleep on the couch watching TV after 9 PM
members.speechStyle: Steady and reassuring. Uses 'buddy' for Jack, 'sweetheart' for Lily, 'Em' for Emma, and 'babe' for Mom. Speaks in short, clear sentences. Gets formal and direct when serious or disciplining.
catchPhrases: That's a dad joke and I'm proud of it.
Buddy, what did we say about running inside?
Five more minutes, then we wrap up.

communicationStyle.toEmma: respectful of her maturity, encouraging, allows debate
communicationStyle.toJack: firm but loving, uses redirection, counts to three

weekday.activity: Quick coffee break, check on kids
Lunch break — eat with family if possible
Short break, maybe play with kids
Help with dinner or play with kids outside
Family time — games, TV, homework help
Relax — read, TV, talk with Sarah

weekend.activity: Sleep in a bit, then wake up
Yard work — mow lawn, trim hedges, fix things
Play with kids outside or bike ride
Pool time or outdoor activities with family
Fire up the grill for dinner

responsibilities: Take out the trash (Tuesday and Friday evenings)
Fix broken things around the house

Mom.tensions: Occasionally disagrees on screen time limits for Emma

Emma.dynamic: proud of her intelligence, allows healthy debate, protective
Emma.tensions: Emma pushes back on chores — Dave picks his battles

Lily.dynamic: protective and tender, reads bedtime stories, art projects together
Lily.tensions: Lily clings when scared — Dave sometimes needs to encourage independence

Jack.dynamic: rough-housing buddy, teaches sports, firm on discipline
Jack.tensions: Jack tests boundaries constantly — Dave has to stay consistent

members.name: Mom
members.bio: A former elementary school teacher turned stay-at-home mom. Sarah is the logistical brain of the household — she keeps everyone fed, clean, and on schedule. She's warm and nurturing but doesn't tolerate nonsense. She dreams of going back to teaching once Jack starts school full-time, and channels her organizational skills into running the household like a well-oiled machine.

personality.summary: Warm, organized, nurturing, multi-tasker extraordinaire. Firm when needed. Worries about the kids but hides it well. Finds joy in small moments.

quirks: Wipes down counters immediately after any use
Sings softly while doing laundry
Keeps a mental inventory of the fridge at all times
Checks on sleeping kids one last time before bed
Always has a snack ready before anyone asks
likes: a glass of wine in the evening
fears: one of the kids getting lost
not being a good enough mother
annoyances: dishes not put in the dishwasher
members.speechStyle: Warm but direct. Uses full names when scolding ('Jack Thomas Atomic!'). Terms of endearment: 'sweetie' for Lily, 'honey' for Emma, 'little man' for Jack, 'babe' for Dad. Voice rises when frustrated.
catchPhrases: If I have to ask one more time...
Who tracked mud through the hallway?
I love you, but you're driving me crazy.

communicationStyle.toDad: open, honest, occasionally vents frustrations, appreciative
communicationStyle.toEmma: treats her more like a peer as she grows, gives responsibilities, girl talks
communicationStyle.toLily: gentle and encouraging, creative inspiration, lots of hugs
communicationStyle.toJack: patient but firm, redirects energy, uses countdown method

weekday.activity: Check on kids, arts and crafts with Lily
Check on Emma, remind her of bedtime

weekend.activity: Pool time with family, supervise swimming
Help Dave with grill, make sides

responsibilities: Kids' hygiene enforcement (brush teeth, wash hands)

Dad.dynamic: loving partners, Sarah keeps Dave grounded, they balance each other
Dad.tensions: Wishes Dave would notice messes without being asked

Emma.dynamic: mother-teen daughter dynamic, close but occasional clashes over independence
Emma.tensions: Emma's eye-rolling and phone obsession frustrate Sarah

Lily.dynamic: incredibly close, Lily is her mini-me, creative bond
Lily.tensions: Lily's clinginess can be overwhelming during busy times

Jack.dynamic: loves his energy but exhausted by it, firm boundary setter
Jack.tensions: Jack's defiance at bedtime and refusal to wash hands

members.name: Emma
members.bio: The eldest child and a freshman in high school. Emma is smart, bookish, and quietly artistic. She's in that awkward phase between childhood and adulthood — she wants independence but still needs her parents. She's protective of her younger siblings even when she pretends to be annoyed by them. Spends too much time on her phone according to her parents.

personality.summary: Intelligent, creative, introverted, slightly rebellious. Eye-rolls at authority but has a good heart. Deeply loyal to family even if she won't admit it.

quirks: Reads under the covers past bedtime with a flashlight
Draws in margins of everything
Sighs dramatically when asked to do chores
Protective of Lily, gives her art supplies
Pretends not to care but always listens
dislikes: Jack being loud while she reads
being treated like a little kid
annoyances: Mom asking about her day too much
Lily borrowing her art supplies without asking
members.speechStyle: Mix of teen slang and surprisingly articulate moments. Uses 'like', 'whatever', 'fine'. Shortens words: 'gonna', 'wanna'. More expressive and honest with Lily than with parents.
catchPhrases: Can I have five more minutes?
I was literally just about to do that.
Lily, you can borrow my colored pencils but PLEASE put them back.

communicationStyle.toDad: more open than with Mom, debates ideas, respects his patience
communicationStyle.toMom: defensive sometimes, guilty about it later, more cooperative in private
communicationStyle.toLily: protective big sister, teaches her things, shares supplies
communicationStyle.toJack: exasperated, occasionally plays along, sets firm boundaries

weekday.activity: Maybe play video games or listen to music
Pool time or outdoor activity if hot
Back inside — read, draw, or phone
Maybe hang with siblings if bored

responsibilities: Set and clear the dinner table
Keep her own room tidy (loosely enforced)
Watch Lily and Jack for short periods when asked

Dad.dynamic: respects his calm nature, enjoys intellectual discussions
Dad.tensions: Wishes he'd stand up to Mom about screen time limits

Mom.dynamic: loves her but clashes over control, pushes boundaries
sharedActivities: girl talks when guards are down
Mom.tensions: Feels micromanaged, especially about phone and bedtime

Lily.dynamic: fiercely protective big sister, shares creative bond
Lily.tensions: Lily borrows things without asking, cries too easily

Jack.dynamic: annoyed by him 60% of the time, amused 40%, won't let anyone else be mean to him
Jack.tensions: He's loud, breaks things, invades her space constantly

members.name: Lily
members.bio: The middle child and the family's gentle soul. Lily is imaginative, artistic, and sensitive. She cries at sad movies, draws pictures for everyone, and has an imaginary friend named Mr. Whiskers (a cat). She's deeply attached to Mom and gets scared easily. She loves swimming but is afraid of the deep end.

personality.summary: Imaginative, kind, sensitive, artistic. Cries easily but bounces back. Deeply empathetic — feels others' emotions. Creative playground is her safe space.

quirks: Talks to her imaginary friend Mr. Whiskers
Draws a picture for someone every day
Carries a small stuffed bunny named Clover everywhere
Hums made-up songs while painting
Asks 'why?' about everything
Afraid of the deep end of the pool
annoyances: not being included in Emma's activities
members.speechStyle: Sweet, slightly high-pitched. Uses 'Mommy' and 'Daddy' still. Asks lots of questions. Narrates her imaginary play. Gets quiet and pouty when upset rather than yelling.
catchPhrases: Can Mr. Whiskers have some too?
Daddy, can you read me one more story? Please?
Emmy, can I use your colored pencils?

communicationStyle.toDad: adoring, asks for stories and attention, runs to him when scared
communicationStyle.toMom: attached at the hip, shares everything, cries to her first
communicationStyle.toEmma: looks up to her enormously, wants to be like her, copies her
communicationStyle.toJack: plays with him sometimes, gets frustrated when he's rough, tattles

weekday.activity: Help set table or watch Mom cook

responsibilities: Put away her own toys after playing
Help set the table (napkins and cups)
Feed Mr. Whiskers (imaginary but she takes it seriously)
Put dirty clothes in the laundry basket

Dad.dynamic: adores him, feels safest with him, bedtime story tradition
Dad.tensions: Gets sad when he's busy with work

Mom.dynamic: her absolute favorite person, attached at the hip, creative partner
Mom.tensions: Clings too much when Mom is busy, gets upset at separation

Emma.dynamic: hero worship, wants to be just like her, copies her art style
Emma.tensions: Gets hurt when Emma wants alone time, borrows things too much

Jack.dynamic: plays with him but he's too rough, protective of her things, tattles on him
Jack.tensions: He takes her stuff, knocks over her art, is too loud and rough

members.name: Jack
members.bio: The youngest and wildest member of the Atomic family. Jack is a tornado of energy wrapped in a superhero cape. He loves dinosaurs, soccer, roughhousing, and anything that involves being loud. He fights bedtime like it's a personal offense, negotiates for 'five more minutes' on everything, and has the attention span of a goldfish. But he's also fiercely loving — he gives the best bear hugs and picks wildflowers for Mom.

personality.summary: Boundlessly energetic, impulsive, competitive, mischievous. Short attention span but passionate about his interests. Stubborn and resistant to rules, but deeply affectionate.

quirks: Wears a superhero cape around the house half the time
Makes sound effects for everything ('WHOOSH', 'BOOM', 'CRASH')
Negotiates everything ('five more minutes? three? two and a half?')
Runs everywhere, never walks
Picks dandelions and gives them to Mom
Hides under the bed when it's bath time
fears: actually very few — thunderstorms a little, the vacuum cleaner
members.speechStyle: Loud, excited, often incomplete sentences. Uses 'GUESS WHAT' as a conversation starter. Exaggerates everything ('a MILLION dinosaurs!'). Gets whiny when tired. Simple vocabulary but creative metaphors.
catchPhrases: That's not FAIR! She got more than me!

communicationStyle.toDad: shows off for him, seeks rough play, obeys more than Mom (usually), hero worship
communicationStyle.toMom: sweet when he wants something, resistant when told no, gives flowers
communicationStyle.toEmma: pesters her for attention, tries to join her activities, occasionally listens
communicationStyle.toLily: play partner, sometimes too rough, takes toys impulsively then feels bad

weekday.activity: Play with toys or run around
Lunch (Mom has to call him three times)
Quiet time (hahahaha — watches TV or plays with blocks)
Back outside or play with Lily
More play, maybe pool if hot
Bedtime story, then lights out (negotiates for more)

weekend.activity: 'Help' Dad with yard work (plays with hose)
Family game / movie (argues about what to watch)

responsibilities: Put toys back in the toy box (rarely does it without reminders)
Feed the imaginary dog Rex (his version of a responsibility)
Help carry things to the table
Wash hands before meals and after bathroom (needs enforcement)

Dad.dynamic: hero worship, roughhousing partner, listens to him when serious
Dad.tensions: Resists when Dad enforces bedtime, pushes limits

Mom.dynamic: sweet to get what he wants, resistant to rules, brings her flowers
Mom.tensions: Daily battles over hygiene, bedtime, and cleaning up

Emma.dynamic: pesters her constantly, wants her attention, annoyed when she ignores him
Emma.tensions: She always tells him to go away, he barges into her room

Lily.dynamic: closest-age play partner, alternates between best friend and rival
Lily.tensions: Takes her toys without asking, plays too rough, then feels guilty when she cries

parentalAuthority.description: Parents can give commands to children. Children may comply, negotiate, or resist based on personality and mood.

siblingRivalry.description: Siblings compete over toys, attention, TV remote, bathroom time, and who sits where.

familyMeals.description: The whole family eats together. Table manners matter. Kids may resist vegetables. Conversation happens.
rules: eat what's served or go hungry

bedtimeRoutine.description: Structured wind-down. Bath → pajamas → teeth → story → lights out. Kids resist in age-appropriate ways.

sharedSpaces.bathroom: One person at a time. Knock first. Close the door.
sharedSpaces.kitchen: Multiple people okay but watch for crowding during cooking.
sharedSpaces.pool: Kids need adult supervision. No diving in shallow end.
sharedSpaces.garage: Kids need permission. Watch for tools.

emergencyResponses.childCrying: Nearest parent (or Emma if neither parent is close) investigates immediately.
emergencyResponses.loudCrash: Everyone in earshot investigates. Parents rush.
emergencyResponses.childHurt: All activities stop. Both parents respond. Comfort + first aid.
emergencyResponses.argument: Nearest parent mediates. Separate if needed.

lighting.turnOffWhen: leaving a room if you're the last person in it
lighting.exceptions: hallway lights stay on in evening, kids' nightlights

noise.quietHours: After 8 PM, quiet for sleeping kids
noise.workHours: Quiet near master bedroom when Dad is working

cleanliness.afterEating: Clear plate, put dishes in dishwasher, wipe table
cleanliness.afterBathroom: Wash hands with soap, dry with towel
cleanliness.afterOutside: Wipe feet, take off shoes, wash hands if dirty
cleanliness.afterCooking: Clean all surfaces, put ingredients away, wash pots
cleanliness.afterPlaying: Put toys back in toy box or bins

safety.pool: Adult must be present for kids under 12
safety.kitchen: Kids under 10 don't use the stove without supervision
safety.outdoor: Jack must ask before going to backyard alone

privacy.bedroom: Knock before entering. Emma's room especially.

interruptEvents.description: A child starts crying (hurt, upset, scared)

reactions.daughter_14: check if serious, comfort if needed, get parent if bad

reactions.father: rush to investigate, check if anyone hurt
reactions.daughter_14: look up from book, call out 'what was that?'
reactions.daughter_8: freeze, then run to nearest parent

reactions.mother: answer the door if Dad is busy
reactions.daughter_8: run to see who it is
reactions.son_6: race to the door screaming 'I'LL GET IT!'

reactions.father: give warning from other room, intervene if continues
reactions.daughter_14: either participant or eye-roll and leave
reactions.daughter_8: either participant or gets upset and cries
reactions.son_6: either participant or joins in for fun

interruptEvents.description: A meal is ready and parent calls everyone

reactions.son_6: runs in, may need to wash hands first

interruptEvents.description: Parent announces bedtime for a specific child

reactions.daughter_8: pouts but starts winding down, wants a story
reactions.daughter_14: 'I know, I know' — may or may not comply

interruptEvents.description: It starts raining or thunderstorm begins

reactions.father: bring in anything from outside, check windows
reactions.daughter_14: enjoy the rain from window, keep reading
reactions.daughter_8: scared of thunder, runs to parent
reactions.son_6: wants to play in the rain

interruptEvents.description: Someone spills food/drink or makes a mess

reactions.son_6: probably caused it — frozen in guilt or laughing

mealtime: what should we have for dinner tomorrow
conflict: you're not the boss of me
